title: cercocarpus betuloides subsp. blancheae
intro: Cercocarpus betuloides subsp. blancheae is a plant species that belongs to the family Rosaceae and is also known as Mountain Mahogany. Native to the southwestern United States, this deciduous shrub or small tree has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's leaves and bark are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and supporting digestive health. Herbal preparations of Cercocarpus betuloides subsp. blancheae can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat a variety of ailments, including respiratory issues, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal problems.
